content: Punycode local part is not supported

comment.text: The translation key "PunycodeLocalPart" indicates an error message that is used when a local part of an email address cannot be converted to Punycode. This error typically appears in UI elements such as dialog boxes or input fields where email addresses are entered. The phrase translates to the user-friendly error description "Punycode local part is not supported".

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The Arabic translation contains a grammatical gender mismatch. 'الجزء' (part) is a masculine noun, but the adjective 'مدعومة' is feminine. It should be 'مدعوم'.

The translation 'Lokal yolda icazə verilməyən simvollardan istifadə olunur' means 'Unauthorized characters are used in the local path', which is technically incorrect. In the context of email addresses, 'local part' refers to the part before the '@' symbol, not a file system 'path'. Additionally, it fails to mention 'Punycode'.

The Bulgarian translation 'Местните части на Punycode' means 'The local parts of Punycode', which is technically incorrect. In the context of email addresses, 'local part' refers to the part before the '@' symbol. The translation should specify that it is the local part of the address that cannot be in Punycode format.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: Punycode в локалната част не се поддържа

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The translation refers to 'Punycode domain names' (verkkotunnuksia) instead of the 'Punycode local part' (the part of the email before the @ symbol).

The Japanese translation uses 'には' (ni wa), which makes the sentence mean 'As for the Punycode local part, it is not supported' (implying the part itself is the subject being unsupported), whereas the English 'Punycode local part is not supported' is a direct statement of a condition/feature limitation. More importantly, the grammar 'には対応されていません' is slightly awkward for a system error message; 'は' should be used to indicate the subject of the error.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: ピュニコードのローカルパートはサポートされていません

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The Lao translation 'ພື້ນທີ່ບໍ່ຮອງຮັບ Punycode' means 'Punycode area is not supported' or 'Punycode space is not supported'. It fails to specify that the 'local part' (the part of the email before the @ symbol) is the issue, which is the core technical meaning of the error.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: ສ່ວນຂອງ Local Part ທີ່ເປັນ Punycode ແມ່ນບໍ່ຮອງຮັບ

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The translation incorrectly identifies the 'local part' as 'Identyfikator użytkownika' (Username). In the context of email addresses, 'local part' refers to the part before the '@' symbol, not the user identifier/username.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: Część lokalna Punycode nie jest obsługiwana

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The term 'peça local' is a literal and incorrect translation for the technical term 'local part' in the context of an email address. In Portuguese technical terminology for email protocols, 'parte local' is the correct term.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: A parte local em Punycode não é suportada

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The term 'peça local' is a literal and incorrect translation of 'local part' in the context of an email address. In technical Portuguese, 'local part' refers to the part of the email before the '@' symbol and should be translated as 'parte local'.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: A parte local em Punycode não é suportada

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The translation 'Имя почтового ящика содержит запрещенные символы' (The mailbox name contains prohibited characters) is a generic error message that does not specify the technical cause (Punycode local part). It conflates a specific technical limitation with a general validation error.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: Локальная часть в формате Punycode не поддерживается

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The translation 'ස්ථානීය කොටසට අන්තර්ජාතික කේත සහාය නොදක්වයි' translates to 'International code does not support the local part', which reverses the logic of the English sentence. The English states that the 'Punycode local part' (the subject) is not supported, whereas the Sinhala version implies the code is what doesn't support the part. Additionally, 'Punycode' is a specific technical term that should ideally be transliterated or kept as is, rather than being translated as 'International code' (අන්තර්ජාතික කේත).
The term 'Punycode' was incorrectly transliterated as 'панікоді' (panikodi), which sounds like 'panic' in Ukrainian, instead of the technical term 'Punycode'.
